questions.question: In the context of Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) design, what is a 'standard cell'?
options: A pre-designed and pre-characterized basic logic building block (e.g., NAND gate, D flip-flop) available in a library.
A specialized analog circuit used for power delivery to critical components.
A memory module with configurable size and access time, typically used for caches.
A high-speed communication interface block for off-chip data transfer.
A diagnostic circuit for monitoring the operating temperature of the chip.

questions.question: Which cache replacement policy selects the cache block for eviction that has not been referenced for the longest period of time?
options: Least Recently Used (LRU)
First-In, First-Out (FIFO)
Random Replacement (RR)
Least Frequently Used (LFU)
Most Recently Used (MRU)

questions.question: In Static Timing Analysis (STA) for a flip-flop, what does a 'hold time violation' primarily indicate?
options: The data input to the flip-flop changed too soon after the active clock edge, failing to hold the previous data stable long enough.
The data input arrived too late before the active clock edge, failing to meet the setup requirement.
The clock signal itself is unstable or has excessive jitter.
The output of the flip-flop cannot propagate to the next sequential element within the clock period.
The reset signal was not de-asserted in time before the clock edge.

questions.question: In Design for Testability (DFT) for digital ICs, what is the primary purpose of Automatic Test Pattern Generation (ATPG)?
options: To automatically generate the Register Transfer Level (RTL) code from high-level specifications.
To create a set of input stimuli (test vectors) that can detect a high percentage of potential manufacturing defects in the circuit.
To verify the functional correctness of the design against its behavioral model.
To synthesize the gate-level netlist from the RTL code, optimizing for area and performance.
To estimate the power consumption of the chip under various operational scenarios.

questions.question: In multi-processor computer systems, what is the primary role of an 'atomic operation' (e.g., atomic increment, compare-and-swap)?
options: To enhance the performance of single-threaded applications by bypassing cache memory.
To ensure that an operation on shared memory appears to be instantaneous and indivisible to all other processors, preventing data corruption from concurrent access.
To optimize the instruction fetch and decode stages of the CPU pipeline.
To reduce the number of instructions required to perform complex mathematical computations.
To manage the allocation and deallocation of virtual memory pages.
